mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override earnings growth, causing sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: During high inflation regimes, rising CPI forces the Federal Reserve to delay rate hikes, creating a temporary liquidity premium that pushes S&P 500 returns positive over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns do not materialize when CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3% monthly, or the signal reverses direction during high inflation periods with Rank IC below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate capacity utilization, which directly translates to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 20-day return direction with a Rank IC above 0.03, or positive industrial production changes coincide with negative equity returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain exceeding 200,000 signals robust labor demand and wage growth that fuels consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200k in a single month
failure_condition: SPX delivers negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of months following a 200k payroll gain, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates while inflation remains elevated, stagflation dynamics compress profit margins and trigger risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combination of falling GDPC1 and rising CPIAUCSL yields positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment, a federal funds rate below 2.5% sustains cheap credit conditions and encourages equity risk-taking, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS stays below 2.5%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy with Rank IC below 0.01 during accommodative phases.
